Plot loss ratio projection as a triangle heatmap

Source: R/lr-vis.R
plot_triangle.LRFit.Rd

Visualise an "LRFit" object as a triangle-style heatmap of cumulative loss ratios. Observed and projected cells are distinguished by border style.

Usage

# S3 method for class 'LRFit'
plot_triangle(
  x,
  what = c("full", "pred"),
  label_style = c("value", "detail"),
  label_args = list(),
  show_maturity = TRUE,
  digits = 0,
  amount_divisor = 1e+08,
  theme = c("view", "save", "shiny"),
  nrow = NULL,
  ncol = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

x

An object of class "LRFit".

what

One of "full" (observed + projected) or "pred" (projected cells only). Default is "full".

label_style

One of "value" (lr only) or "detail" (lr with loss/exposure amounts). Default is "value".

label_args

Named list of label appearance arguments.

show_maturity

Logical; if TRUE, show maturity line. Default is TRUE.

digits

Number of decimal places for lr display. Default is 0.

amount_divisor

Numeric divisor for amount display in "detail" mode. Default is 1e8.

theme

Theme string.

nrow, ncol

Facet dimensions.

...

Additional arguments passed to .switch_theme().

Value

A ggplot object.
